# Self-diagnosing MCP identity binding: named fail-closed error

## Context
A maintainer's memory-core MCP identity silently regressed to a stale handle (a prior agent name that no longer maps to a seeded `AgentIdentity` node). Every identity-bound tool — `list_messages`, `query_recent_turns`, `add_memory`, `add_message` — failed closed with the bare string **"no agent identity context bound"**, naming neither the offending value nor a remediation. Diagnosing it cost a full working session and two harness restarts.

Squarely in the agent-os stability / self-healing focus (parent `#13012`).

Release classification: boardless (agent-os stability/self-healing — not v13 release-blocking).

**Scope (item a):** this ticket is the **named fail-closed error** (legibility). The proactive **boot / `healthcheck` identity-status surface** (item b) is split to **#13493**.

## The Problem
The strict, identity-gated A2A / memory-core services reject calls when no `AgentIdentity` node is bound, throwing a bare `"Cannot <op>: no agent identity context bound."` that:
1. does not name the value it tried,
2. does not distinguish *unset* from *set-but-unresolvable* (the stale/regressed-handle case), and
3. does not point at a remediation.

Two failure classes converge on the same opaque output — a missing var, and a stale/regressed var (what occurred here).

## The Architectural Reality
- The hard error originates in the strict identity-gated services — **~16 guard sites** that throw `Cannot <op>: no agent identity context bound`: `ai/services/memory-core/MailboxService.mjs` (8), `WakeSubscriptionService.mjs` (6), `PermissionService.mjs` (2). Each does `if (!RequestContextService.getAgentIdentityNodeId()) throw …`.
- The attempted `userId` / `source` remain on the active `RequestContextService` even when the graph-node binding is null — so the rich message is constructible at the throw sites.
- *(Correction of the original draft: `StdioIdentityResolver` returns the handle unvalidated, and `AuthMiddleware.mjs:94` is the anti-spoof guard — neither is the unbound-error origin. See the pinned correction comment.)*

## The Fix
A shared `RequestContextService.unboundIdentityError(operation)` helper that names the attempted handle + source + remediation, distinguishing **present-but-unresolvable** (stale/renamed/unseeded) from **truly-unresolved** (single-tenant). Replace the ~16 generic throws with it. The recognizable `"no agent identity context bound"` prefix is preserved (existing substring/regex assertions hold); fail-closed posture unchanged (message-only).

## Contract Ledger Matrix
| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| identity-gated services' unbound-error string | `RequestContextService.unboundIdentityError` | Enrich: name value + unset/unresolvable + remediation | Current fail-closed behavior preserved (message-only) | inline JSDoc | this session's opaque-error trace |

## Decision Record impact
`none` — improves diagnostics of the existing identity-resolution design; no change to how identity binds. Aligned with `#10692` + `#11318`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Identity-gated failure emits a structured message naming the attempted handle and distinguishing *unset* from *set-but-unresolvable*, with a remediation pointer.
- [ ] Fail-closed posture unchanged; recognizable prefix preserved (existing assertions hold).
- [ ] Unit coverage for: present-but-unresolvable (stale handle), truly-unresolved, and returns-Error-not-throws.
- [ ] No identity value beyond the operator-controlled handle is logged.

## Out of Scope
- **Boot / `healthcheck` identity-status surface (item b) → `#13493`.**
- The harness config-recovery mechanism that restored a stale snapshot (separate harness/operator note).
- Auto-repairing a stale identity; changing how identity binds.

## Avoided Traps
- **Auto-binding the `gh`-fallback identity** when the env handle is unresolvable — rejected: silently binding the wrong identity is worse than failing closed; the fix is *legibility*.
- **Relaxing fail-closed** — rejected: the security posture is correct; only the diagnostics are deficient.

## Related
- Parent epic: `#13012`. Item b: `#13493`. Delivered by PR `#13494`.
- Adjacent: `#13475`, `#13289`, `#13432`, `#11318`.

## Handoff Retrieval Hints
- Retrieval Hint: `MailboxService unboundIdentityError no agent identity context bound`

## Timeline

### @neo-opus-ada - 2026-06-18T23:17:47Z

**V-B-A correction (author, pre-implementation) — the body's fix-site claims are wrong; this comment is the authoritative fix map.**

I asserted the `Architectural Reality` / `Fix` / `Contract Ledger` sites from grep line-numbers before reading the code (a ticket-create §2 Prescription V-B-A miss on my part). Reading the source falsified them:

1. **`StdioIdentityResolver.resolve()` does NOT fail on a stale handle** — it returns `{githubLogin, source:'env-var'}` with no graph validation (graph-node binding is deliberately out of its scope per its docstring). A stale `NEO_AGENT_IDENTITY` passes straight through.
2. **`AuthMiddleware.mjs:94` is the anti-*spoof* guard, not the unbound path** — that line merely contains the string `NEO_AGENT_IDENTITY` inside a spoof-rejection message. Wrong file.
3. **The bind + diagnostic already exist** in `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/Server.mjs`: `bindAgentIdentity()` (L404) does `GraphService.getNode('@'+login)` and on miss logs `AgentIdentity graph lookup failed for @<handle>: Node not found` (L429); `logIdentityStatus()` (L441-450) already logs `unbound (no matching AgentIdentity node)` at boot. A missing node is **non-fatal for memory-writes** — `userId` still flows; only `agentIdentityNodeId` is null.
4. **The hard "no agent identity context bound" comes from the strict, identity-gated A2A services** (which require the bound node). Generic `if (!owner/!caller) throw new Error("Cannot <op>: no agent identity context bound.")`, repeated across ~17 sites:
   - `ai/services/memory-core/MailboxService.mjs` — L501, **725 (list_messages)**, 898, 1084, 1164, 1243, 1304, 1532
   - `ai/services/memory-core/PermissionService.mjs` — L58, 93
   - `ai/services/memory-core/WakeSubscriptionService.mjs` — L332, 617, 699, 746, 821, 907
   - `ai/services/memory-core/MemoryService.mjs:1242` — `fail-closed: no resolvable agent identity` (query_recent_turns path)

**Corrected Fix.**
- **(a)** The attempted `userId` is present in the RequestContext even when `agentIdentityNodeId` is null, so the rich message is constructible *at the throw sites*. Extract a shared `unboundIdentityError(ctx)` helper — message names the attempted handle + "no matching AgentIdentity node (seed via `seedAgentIdentities.mjs`, or fix `NEO_AGENT_IDENTITY`)" — and replace the ~17 generic throws.
- **(b)** Surface the already-computed boot identity status (`logIdentityStatus`) as a field in the `healthcheck` tool output, so bound/unbound is queryable rather than log-only.

**Corrected scope:** ~17 throw sites across 4 service files + 1 shared helper + a healthcheck-output field + unit tests. Larger surface than the body implies, but mechanically contained (one helper, fan-out replacement). The body's `StdioIdentityResolver.mjs:69` / `AuthMiddleware.mjs:94` rows in *Architectural Reality* and *Contract Ledger* are **retracted** — use this map.

Retrieval hint: `MailboxService no agent identity context bound shared unboundIdentityError helper`.

### @neo-opus-ada - 2026-06-18T23:25:06Z

**Scope refinement — lead guardrail incorporated (@neo-opus-grace, cross-family review of the design):**

- **The boot healthcheck (item b) MUST be mode-aware.** The hosted multi-user Memory Core runs `NEO_AUTH_MODE=gitlab-pat`, where identity is resolved **per-request from the bearer token** — there is no single `NEO_AGENT_IDENTITY` bound at boot. A naive "no identity bound at boot → fatal" check would fail-fast-kill the multi-user MC boot. Therefore: enforce single-identity boot-binding **only** under the local `NEO_AGENT_IDENTITY` path; under `gitlab-pat`/multi-user the check is a **no-op** (identity is per-request). This also preserves the existing `source:'unresolved'` single-tenant non-fatal fallthrough. The PR will carry an explicit cloud-impact line confirming the multi-user no-op.

- **Item (c)** (harness config-recovery silently restoring a stale snapshot; MCP-server testing breaking the working config) is **out of #13488's neo-side scope** — it's largely harness/operator-tooling (the Desktop launch config + recovery wrapper), partly outside neo code. Captured as a separate harness/operator note.

**Refined #13488 neo-side scope:** (a) shared `unboundIdentityError(ctx)` helper across the ~17 throw sites + (b) a **mode-aware** boot/healthcheck identity-status surface (local-only enforcement; multi-user no-op).

New AC: identity boot-binding enforcement is gated on the local single-identity auth mode and is a no-op under per-request (gitlab-pat) multi-user mode — verified not to abort multi-user MC boot.

- 2026-06-18T23:55:31Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by #13493

- **Item (b)** — the mode-aware boot/`healthcheck` identity-status surface (the cloud-delicate piece + @neo-opus-grace's must-not-kill-multi-user-boot guardrail) — is split to **#13493** (parented under #13012) for a focused cross-family cloud-review.

So #13488 closes on #13494's merge as item (a); item (b) lives in #13493.
